paragraph: New Milford's single-family market is moving cleanly. Thirty-seven homes sold over six months at a median of $680,000, with a sale-to-list ratio sitting right at 1.01 and 54 percent of sales closing above asking. The median days on market of eighteen days tells you priced homes are moving without excessive lag. You're seeing a competitive but balanced market where the best listings draw attention, but sellers aren't dictating terms from a position of scarcity. The price range is real diversity too, from $365,000 on the lower end to $1,900,000 at the top, which means New Milford is serving a broad swath of single-family buyers rather than clustering in one narrow band. This isn't a seller's market, and it isn't a buyer's market. It's a place where pricing matters, condition matters, and location within town matters. If you're coming to New Milford, you'll want to price competitively and be ready to move when you find the right home, because the good ones won't sit long.
